pattern: Binary palindromes are sparse but fixed in advance (they never depend on the query), so precomputing every one of them up to a safe ceiling and binary-searching each query against that sorted list beats searching outward from scratch every time.
hints: Binary palindromes are a small, fixed set of numbers you could list out ahead of time -- they don't depend on any particular query value.
Generate every binary palindrome up to a comfortably large ceiling once (checking whether a number's binary string reads the same forwards and backwards is cheap), and keep that list sorted.

For each query value, binary-search the sorted palindrome list for where it would be inserted, then compare only the neighboring one or two candidates around that position -- one of them is guaranteed to be the closest.

edge_inputs.description: smallest allowed value, already a palindrome
a power of two sits exactly halfway between two nearby palindromes
several already-palindromic binary values need zero operations
repeated identical values should all cost the same

stress.description: many random values across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 5000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

complexity.time: O(n log P) after an O(P) one-time precompute (P ~ 20000)
